messages.content:
You are a senior infrastructure capacity engineer. Assess whether Racing Point eSports can handle v29.0 Meshed Intelligence without degrading game performance.

## CURRENT HARDWARE

### Server (.23)
- CPU: 24 logical processors
- RAM: 64 GB (65 GB visible)
- Storage: C: 1.4 TB free / 2 TB total, D+E+Z: 1.5 TB free
- Current racecontrol.exe: 28 MB RAM
- Current DB: racecontrol.db 27 MB, telemetry.db 20 KB
- Services: racecontrol (:8080), 3 Next.js apps (:3200, :3201, :3300)

### 8 Gaming Pods (identical)
- GPU: RTX 4070 (16 GB VRAM)
- Current GPU load (idle): 0%, 1.8 GB / 16.3 GB VRAM, 48°C
- Current rc-agent.exe: 27 MB RAM
- Runs: rc-agent (:8090), rc-sentry (:8091), Edge browser, game processes
- Triple monitor NVIDIA Surround (5760x1080 or similar)
- Games: AC, ACC, F1 25, iRacing, LMU, rFactor2, ForzaHorizon5

### POS PC (.20)
- WiFi connected, Edge kiosk, rc-pos-agent

### James (.27, this machine)
- RTX 4070, Ollama (qwen2.5:3b + llama3.1:8b)
- go2rtc (13 RTSP cameras), rc-sentry-ai (face detection)

### Network
- TP-Link router, all on 192.168.31.x LAN
- Tailscale mesh VPN overlay

## v29.0 NEW OVERHEAD

### Server-Side (6 new background tasks)
| Task | Interval | Per-Cycle Work |
|------|----------|---------------|
| anomaly_scanner | 60s | 8 SQL queries (latest telemetry per pod) |
| maintenance_scheduler | 1hr | Hourly aggregation + daily cleanup at 03:00 IST |
| alert_checker | 30min | 3 SQL queries against daily_business_metrics |
| business_aggregator | 1hr | 3 SELECT + 1 UPSERT on billing_sessions/cafe_orders |
| data_collector | 15min | 4 SQL queries + RUL threshold check |
| telemetry_writer | 1s batch | INSERT batch from WS telemetry buffer |

### Pod-Side (1 new interval per pod)
| Task | Interval | Work |
|------|----------|------|
| hw_telemetry_interval | 60s | 1x nvidia-smi (~50ms), sysinfo refresh (~10ms), WS send (~1KB) |
| PowerShell (cached) | 5min | Get-WinEvent + Get-CimInstance (~200ms, results cached) |
| TCP ping to server | 60s | ~5ms |

### Database Growth
- hardware_telemetry: ~5.5 MB/day (8 pods × 1 row/min × 500B)
- 7-day retention policy auto-cleanup → MAX ~40 MB raw telemetry
- Aggregates: ~0.8 MB retained (30-day hourly + 90-day daily)
- Other tables: negligible (<1 MB/year)

### Network
- Pod→Server: +1 KB/min/pod via existing WS = +11.5 MB/day total
- Server→Ollama: ~10 KB/day (5 diagnosis calls)
- Server→WhatsApp: ~2.5 KB/day
- Server→Cloud sync: ~120 KB/day

## GAME PERFORMANCE DURING ACTIVE SESSION

When a customer is racing, the pod runs:
- Game process (AC/ACC/F1/etc) using 80-100% GPU, 4-8 GB VRAM, high CPU
- ConspitLink (wheel/pedal telemetry bridge)
- rc-agent monitoring (heartbeat, driving detection, billing)
- Edge browser (lock screen or overlay — minimized during game)

The NEW hw_telemetry_interval runs EVERY 60 seconds including during gameplay.

## QUESTIONS TO ANSWER

1. Will the 60s nvidia-smi call on pods cause frame drops during racing? (nvidia-smi can briefly lock the GPU driver)
2. Will the PowerShell subprocess calls cause micro-stutters?
3. Can the server handle 8 additional WS telemetry messages/minute on top of existing heartbeats?
4. Will 6 new background SQL tasks cause contention with billing_fsm (the most latency-sensitive path)?
5. Will telemetry.db growing to 40 MB cause WAL checkpoint stalls?
6. Is the TP-Link router sufficient for the additional 11.5 MB/day traffic?
7. Are there any resource bottlenecks that could cascade during peak hours (8 pods active + all background tasks)?

Provide:
- VERDICT per question (SAFE / RISK / CRITICAL)
- Overall infrastructure VERDICT (CAN HANDLE / AT RISK / CANNOT HANDLE)
- Specific mitigations for any RISK items
- Game performance impact estimate (frame drop risk in ms)
